name: Team Shedd
subject: FWD: Politicians won’t secure the border
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shedd for Attorney General
body:
Friend, Did you see Tiffany’s email? ** ** **We’re only a couple weeks away from filing our petitions to get her on the Republican ballot for Attorney General.** ** ** [Sign her petition on the Secretary of State’s website TODAY and help us send a DECISIVE message to the political establishment that the people of Arizona will no longer tolerate the invasion at our southern border.](http://links.sheddforaz.com/u/click?_t=aef90635dd7e4e83bfd7b3a79f893ad5&_m=f2347510dfaa420b94b47fefe3267db9&_e=mAsJ9blQ4wvji484VQLng28ZLnvqU75MvJL6JqOuUu854VFlLQc0HlEeJBWv-N-qK9slkuK-Hxkwy5UsosFOzZPZ-rDCRKI6mrWI3EPnlAmTi4ovXf_q3jPIE1M1sS5-Qb1gPhtLZLFkcJCHlkJI0-utjaBJd1sFLPtsBKWzgGr0FG_-eSGyUIwWVLNycCu-nG_mX54uObeqYK-6ta-iDerzKY-20279HyCU_Xx03PEXxllSBFSPr3-awtoAzvIE6dg3Xe7Ek08uccTo_dYTPQ%3D%3D) ** ** \- Team Shedd **\-------------------------------Forwarded Email------------------------------------** **From: **Tiffany Shedd <[info@sheddforaz.com](http://links.sheddforaz.com/u/click?_t=aef90635dd7e4e83bfd7b3a79f893ad5&_m=f2347510dfaa420b94b47fefe3267db9&_e=mAsJ9blQ4wvji484VQLng28ZLnvqU75MvJL6JqOuUu-w22jsEdx-x1Nup-zbV9LnF8FwH0VQ2lUotZZAX5qY1cWwrhaoCvZW3MIQ9Jac51XlSUdTDKKIZr4--YQmjMzm7gCsBowrh_8uq2lTHQCWLotwaihArg7kCuvLe_bI-L3vUAreUbzxKDIpR46UenZskXVxwvX3zuxg7yTIwhx2SdkIkH8_ehObb1ykJIaEdIUP7p0SMmWuG8iLUtCxA8uBMmGbHBzC-P8XsYAtnfWCFg%3D%3D)> **Subject: **Politicians won’t secure the border **Date: **March 17, 2022 Friend, For nearly two decades, I have been fighting alongside Arizona’s ranchers to get our elected leaders to do something about the border crisis. **And every year they do the same thing –**** they come down to the border for a photo op, promptly leave, and get NOTHING done.** Meanwhile, border ranchers and families like mine are forced to live with the fallout of their inaction. We watch the drug smugglers, human traffickers, and cartels that employ them pour across our lands in record numbers. They steal from us, threaten us, and our government lets it happen. **I’ve had enough of the false promises and inaction from politicians.** I am running for Attorney General because I will not watch this crisis continue when I know we can do something about it! **President Trump showed us what a true conservative leader can do when it comes to border security. When the media and political establishment told him he couldn’t build the wall, he did it anyway. And when they lied and tried to paint his immigration policies as inhumane, he NEVER relented. It was refreshing to see a leader who FINALLY PUT AMERICAN FAMILIES FIRST.** That’s the kind of leadership Arizona needs as we face an unprecedented invasion at our southern border, and it’s exactly the kind of leadership I am going to bring to the Attorney General’s office. **Like Trump, I am never going to wake up concerned about what the political establishment or media think. My sole focus is going to be protecting Arizonans, securing our border, and putting an end to the invasion.** In just a couple of weeks, I’ll be filing petition signatures from thousands of Arizona conservatives to get on the ballot as a Republican candidate for Attorney General. [Please sign my petition online before I do and join our fight to secure the border and put Arizona First.](http://links.sheddforaz.com/u/click?_t=aef90635dd7e4e83bfd7b3a79f893ad5&_m=f2347510dfaa420b94b47fefe3267db9&_e=mAsJ9blQ4wvji484VQLng28ZLnvqU75MvJL6JqOuUu8MLAWpLEF1vOpp-JwrJaLA6UjEW2aDHBWNSXHlexm6ArDXafbEQJYC2GcUOLeRgWv34DMJsOCJa13FzzoS9uaWqF18dX7UOVd5ec6bfqVMznmeQVmUlR_GKHzF5HSr9SMZHrno7eX3d__Kr35KRS5eqxPt5SOvDJDQFkhJDKEESRKgwoftgqxwyBb7erSzarL6PpsmmS8By4LI2hCO6fNXt7I6CMh7nWDANPH2iMOxMw%3D%3D) God bless you and God bless Arizona. \- Tiffany Shedd [**CONTRIBUTE**](http://links.sheddforaz.com/u/click?_t=aef90635dd7e4e83bfd7b3a79f893ad5&_m=f2347510dfaa420b94b47fefe3267db9&_e=mAsJ9blQ4wvji484VQLngwz5_ovfEr8hSJ_7TreHrENhVqgQveKcwFtGskPbXBGEIZcXwZd0NSDPUDgLXpwT6ldJZPMBVztg7PF2ic8qrM2pdxyrTC_hILa7-gtYCPC8Y2gXdvql9yLAZ6Sb9gBp_TUJb-TkCfHfOwaJ7j2CkranzLvVORoHNABnFMldQw7fKzC8YMabFyHGA0auYnjgJf0TC3mzxJGdQcHao_dDMdcgwSjIXDhzNf8_gWTS2ZjXp2dZMT31rXbZPMi2OrNdAoTdP9J4GG5nKSKH9RltWf8TygkiybefD68V1XdinZInD6lUV00YnmPrf9KS6yNwDETu0z81rEO6tvqvRC5G0Z-lbNNeTRNgwL831zOjhizd4wSnRlUoG31tqae-8b5QF8wUVdjG8CMgv1VzgY-DWimo7oBvf0F3wvt143jRufR_eT3sJ51Sh1pDfEPXTRXDzwOHU9ARKzLP4D15Yy5RSAs%3D) Paid for by Shedd for Attorney General.

clean_body: Friend, Did you see Tiffany’s email? We’re only a couple weeks away from filing our petitions to get her on the Republican ballot for Attorney General. \- Team Shedd \-------------------------------Forwarded Email------------------------------------ From: Tiffany Shedd <> Subject: Politicians won’t secure the border Date: March 17, 2022 Friend, For nearly two decades, I have been fighting alongside Arizona’s ranchers to get our elected leaders to do something about the border crisis. And every year they do the same thing – they come down to the border for a photo op, promptly leave, and get NOTHING done. Meanwhile, border ranchers and families like mine are forced to live with the fallout of their inaction. We watch the drug smugglers, human traffickers, and cartels that employ them pour across our lands in record numbers. They steal from us, threaten us, and our government lets it happen. I’ve had enough of the false promises and inaction from politicians. I am running for Attorney General because I will not watch this crisis continue when I know we can do something about it! President Trump showed us what a true conservative leader can do when it comes to border security. When the media and political establishment told him he couldn’t build the wall, he did it anyway. And when they lied and tried to paint his immigration policies as inhumane, he NEVER relented. It was refreshing to see a leader who FINALLY PUT AMERICAN FAMILIES FIRST. That’s the kind of leadership Arizona needs as we face an unprecedented invasion at our southern border, and it’s exactly the kind of leadership I am going to bring to the Attorney General’s office. Like Trump, I am never going to wake up concerned about what the political establishment or media think. My sole focus is going to be protecting Arizonans, securing our border, and putting an end to the invasion. In just a couple of weeks, I’ll be filing petition signatures from thousands of Arizona conservatives to get on the ballot as a Republican candidate for Attorney General. God bless you and God bless Arizona. \- Tiffany Shedd Paid for by Shedd for Attorney General. Authorized by Shedd for Attorney General.

body:
When American talking heads spread misinformation, it's not only harmful at home but also abroad.
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43171580/336050329/396464211?refcode=em-logo&source=em220320-1-a&refcode2=443783a0-62a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&contactdata=O1VEEFC0fBQF3Olj5XmgFbCmNZnk+7R03kDCbXpjTnHxkl6WXbwV2yOlA7j6PNtr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODIzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NDM3ODNhMC02MmE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pV6jcJde1FqvExkcA75E5ZnAPR9oYQl6q4G4bJmXE9E=)
Friend —
This week, we learned the Kremlin sent a memo to Russian state media saying it's _"essential to feature Tucker Carlson,"_ who has been spreading misinformation about Putin's horrific war in Ukraine on his own show.
Putin is doing everything he can to hide the truth about his war from the Russian people – and Tucker Carlson is a supporting partner in this effort.
Since the Russian invasion of Ukraine, we have seen a number of corporations take a principled stance, pulling business out of Russia even in the face of financial loss.
It’s time for businesses advertising on Tucker Carlson’s show to take a stand as well – to stop Fox News from profiting off his peddling of Putin’s propaganda.
That is why I am calling on all of Tucker Carlson’s sponsors to stop advertising during his show, and I hope you will join me.
[**Will you add your name below and join me in calling on Tucker Carlson's advertisers to stop supporting his show so long as he continues to be a part of Putin's propaganda machine?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43171583/336050331/-632109860?source=em220320-1-a&refcode2=443783a0-62a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&contactdata=O1VEEFC0fBQF3Olj5XmgFbCmNZnk+7R03kDCbXpjTnHxkl6WXbwV2yOlA7j6PNtr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODIzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NDM3ODNhMC02MmE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pV6jcJde1FqvExkcA75E5ZnAPR9oYQl6q4G4bJmXE9E=&emci=3ff6e15b-a8a7-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&emdi=443783a0-62a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&ceid=2216363)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43171585/336050334/-632109860?source=em220320-1-a&refcode2=443783a0-62a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&contactdata=O1VEEFC0fBQF3Olj5XmgFbCmNZnk+7R03kDCbXpjTnHxkl6WXbwV2yOlA7j6PNtr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODIzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NDM3ODNhMC02MmE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pV6jcJde1FqvExkcA75E5ZnAPR9oYQl6q4G4bJmXE9E=&emci=3ff6e15b-a8a7-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&emdi=443783a0-62a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&ceid=2216363)**
Just think about it – at a moment when Putin is arresting Russian journalists and citizens who are brave enough to speak the truth about his brutal war, the Kremlin is asking Russian state-controlled TV to feature statements from an American commentator. Stunning!
Tucker Carlson has a right to say what he wants to say – as unhinged and untrue as it is – but advertisers also have a right, and indeed a “duty”, to express their disgust with Carlson's decision to spread Putin’s disinformation.
That's why I'm calling on all of Tucker Carlson's sponsors to withdraw their advertising support for his show while he's playing Putin's Puppet. Will you please join me?

| Our fundraising has slowed down significantly since January — and we’re not where we need to be to defeat Kyrsten Sinema in a primary. **We set a goal to raise $5,000 this weekend, and we’re only 32% of the way there. Can you pitch in $10 or more to help us close the gap?** **[Contribute Now](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wsEvCNw9RRN8fJ6P2o2MeSSMEC5VCIW5CXPRWsYU0WNyx5YfTGL-j_7Dw4EccgUi5-4c1skECJEO1pVEyM_SGwH8vlzdeveG2hGfXJAoGmvI4kYDNzWWv3mhPT6Z5iZWroNVa41Uo5vHRvoAEyVYP7Z0pJb799LblnvVQVnOFj2-djEz00foxohy1YJhq5JY9NnBGkPI7FqzYoVQUK5dkJSwJEdVzCRiEceP0th2mV_-K4ViUoIrQfnIllDE-D6LOTmwbF6yxNbtlAKF7N_cuQuJhZeGDK1rYASIhasCNdlXow3DVSLD4oPQ_ltDuZ2RL97_xos7rdlpXYepMfho5I2kDoh3nZnU7_SMkb5T9OPw/3ki/WFrjwGCUSuyKRzzCVwQVeg/h1/F9F0ioEPYIvpgO8PwMS1hwDvwdlrdEqLgFrGf-Qr9I8)**

Peter, we need to follow up on yesterday’s email.
We’re falling behind our goal of raising $5,000 in grassroots donations before the weekend is over, and it’s critical we catch up. Here’s why:
Kyrsten Sinema has over $5 million cash on hand — not to mention corporate-backed super PACs willing to defend her at any cost.
**Our grassroots movement is still strong enough to beat Sinema in a primary.** But we can’t ignore the reality on the ground. If we’re going to go toe-to-toe with Sinema, we need to build up serious grassroots resources now, not later.
Remember what Sinema has done to bring us together in the first place, Peter. Ignoring her constituents. Refusing to negotiate. Blocking lower drug prices on behalf of Big Pharma. Siding with Republicans on the filibuster. Mocking President Biden to a room full of GOP lobbyists. And the list goes on...
Sinema’s proven again and again that she needs to go. But it simply won’t happen without us picking up the pace in our grassroots fundraising.
**[If you can swing it, please rush $10 to help us reach our goal of raising $5,000 by midnight, Peter. We need to hit this goal to get back on track.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wsEvCNw9RRN8fJ6P2o2MeSSMEC5VCIW5CXPRWsYU0WNyx5YfTGL-j_7Dw4EccgUi7NKx5D-pi1UK1R95cP0psnn04Ou8wBvTHKmlVmlGGU2XWN6p51eMI0mid6Ad5zuw5puWKmddo_lSwmeV9VNcp44UtQKit0lwVwHfSWn9ErttAPMA1CB_LXH9basALD8CY0CjH-LOMYgFpHx2WlJNXQWsGCNvgOPR9BBg_lDSAsa7BbSWM60S1roCYDgC0mAwn10YTs3QXBrAM2TtS3W5YYjB7oy6eyCu1iiPNqQ0C-09ooi6NTx_mZUM515K_jbvOUr0h7__6vIxmkgkYJAkA6NtI2CisA2xGPg-HsP1KPhg/3ki/WFrjwGCUSuyKRzzCVwQVeg/h2/ihl704SUT_ZOSlEVVhlDpqC5cDPL7X8i_xUUcH4xrAs)**

name: Brent Hennrich
subject: Is the coffee on?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington
body:
I’ve been thinking this morning. | Peter, I was just having a cup of coffee and thinking about my response to a question a reporter with _The Columbian_ asked me the other day. They asked what this campaign is about -- which is one of those questions you need to pause on for a minute because where do you begin? There's a war about to spill into Europe, it's the third calendar year of a global pandemic, and general malaise has seemingly engulfed all of us while the world's elites carry on and get richer. I don't mean to be all house-is-on-fire, but sheesh. We've got to start doing more than just merely surviving. And that's what I told the reporter. I am running because we need to elect people who actually care and want to end the corruption that hurts the most vulnerable in our communities. We need a leader who is accessible to the people of WA-03, someone who listens and wants to see families do better. When friends and neighbors need help, Amber and I are always ready to put the coffee on and do what we can to support them. That's the kind of values we raise our kids with -- and what this campaign is built on. If you're ready to heal Washington, help me start the week off strong. With our Q1 FEC filing deadline around the corner, we need to raise $8,700 more this week alone. We need all of our neighbors across the district to help us flip WA-03 red to blue. That's why I'm asking you now: Could you send $22 or any amount our way to defeat our out-of-touch GOP incumbent and keep this seat out of the hands of Donald Trump's far-right darling? clean_body: I’ve been thinking this morning. | Peter, I was just having a cup of coffee and thinking about my response to a question a reporter with The Columbian asked me the other day. They asked what this campaign is about -- which is one of those questions you need to pause on for a minute because where do you begin? There's a war about to spill into Europe, it's the third calendar year of a global pandemic, and general malaise has seemingly engulfed all of us while the world's elites carry on and get richer. I don't mean to be all house-is-on-fire, but sheesh. We've got to start doing more than just merely surviving. And that's what I told the reporter. I am running because we need to elect people who actually care and want to end the corruption that hurts the most vulnerable in our communities. We need a leader who is accessible to the people of WA-03, someone who listens and wants to see families do better. When friends and neighbors need help, Amber and I are always ready to put the coffee on and do what we can to support them. That's the kind of values we raise our kids with -- and what this campaign is built on. If you're ready to heal Washington, help me start the week off strong. With our Q1 FEC filing deadline around the corner, we need to raise $8,700 more this week alone. We need all of our neighbors across the district to help us flip WA-03 red to blue. That's why I'm asking you now: Could you send $22 or any amount our way to defeat our out-of-touch GOP incumbent and keep this seat out of the hands of Donald Trump's far-right darling? Pitch in here: Thanks, and hope you have a peaceful end to the weekend. -BH \-- Brent Hennrich Candidate for U.S. Congress, WA-03 | Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington ---|--- (C)2022 Brent Hennrich for Washington, all rights reserved Our mailing address is: Brent Hennrich for Washington PO BOX 820070 Vancouver, WA 98682 We hate to see you go, but if you believe you received this email in error, or you no longer wish to receive our emails you can | | Sent via . Team: Twelve years ago, the Supreme Court handed down its ruling in Citizens United, paving the way for unlimited, anonymous money to be spent in our elections.
The decision has been crippling for our democracy ever since – allowing special interests and billionaires to buy unparalleled influence over everyday, working Americans.
In 2022, billionaires will spend huge sums of money trying to prop up ‘big lie’ supporting Republicans who will decimate voting rights to further their interests.
We need grassroots supporters like you to fight back and join us in calling for a constitutional amendment to overturn Citizens United. [https://action.demsofstate.org/a/overturn-citizens-united]

| | | Friend, VP Kamala Harris has been missing in action on so many fronts... While our borders were left wide open for drug cartels and criminals, Kamala was nowhere to be found - _she could barely even manage a photo op!_ And with the tragedy in Ukraine still ongoing, she isn’t fighting for our energy independence - our VP has instead resigned us to even **HIGHER** prices at the pump. We asked this question about Biden earlier... Now we want to get your thoughts on the performance of the Vice President, too. _**_[Is Kamala Harris completely failing the country?](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xOTEwMjM4NjUwNDc4NjMxNjI5JmM9dzB4NiZlPTAmYj05NDU5MDQ2NTcmZD1kOHY3bTB2.dk3p6zdiibUbbEM7H6thKTrYfEfL2BXw0IRPNJh_OgM)_**_**__****[](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xOTEwMjM4NjUwNDc4NjMxNjI5JmM9dzB4NiZlPTAmYj05NDU5MDQ2NjUmZD1iOGQyajFt.5JQPYazZaxuz0-Cz_P5r6rBdnahzndz0iu6h_axXIwg)**

name: Ron Wyden
subject: Reducing our dependence on foreign oil
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43172890/336054722/2140832878?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ODkzMGNmMC02NmE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aHCAKVk9JFhx6YLHzXOEtxwHiLKNgK721-S-67v3vZk=&emci=f5a32268-37a6-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&emdi=58930cf0-66a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&ceid=9818364)
---

| | The world is watching in horror as events unfold in Ukraine. Russia's unprovoked invasion is an affront to decency, democracy, and human rights. I'm grateful for President Biden's leadership. He has united our allies in imposing aggressive sanctions on Russia that will weaken their war machine and weaken the oligarchy that feeds it. **Russia's unprovoked assault on Ukraine is also a stark reminder of the urgent need for the United States to reduce its dependence on foreign energy from tyrants like Putin, which is why I'm proud to be leading the fight in Congress to ban imports of Russian oil and gas.** Transitioning to a clean energy future is as much a national security issue as it is an environmental one. I'm fighting to make that a reality by overhauling our nation's outdated tax code to throw out tax breaks for Big Oil and replace them with incentives for renewable energy. Increasing America's clean energy production and weaning our country off of dirty fossil fuels will protect our families and our environment for generations to come. **It's time for energy independence from authoritarian regimes like Russia, Saudi Arabia, and from Big Oil. Congress needs to get serious about setting our country on the path to a clean energy future, and I'll be fighting tooth and nail to make that a reality.** [Share on facebook](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43172891/336054723/473474290?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ODkzMGNmMC02NmE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aHCAKVk9JFhx6YLHzXOEtxwHiLKNgK721-S-67v3vZk=&emci=f5a32268-37a6-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&emdi=58930cf0-66a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&ceid=9818364) |

I'm fighting to make that a reality by overhauling our nation's outdated tax code to throw out tax breaks for Big Oil and replace them with incentives for renewable energy. Increasing America's clean energy production and weaning our country off of dirty fossil fuels will protect our families and our environment for generations to come. It's time for energy independence from authoritarian regimes like Russia, Saudi Arabia, and from Big Oil. Congress needs to get serious about setting our country on the path to a clean energy future, and I'll be fighting tooth and nail to make that a reality. | ---|---|--- | | Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate. ---|--- | This email was sent by Wyden for Senate P.O. Box 3498 Portland, OR 97208 United States Email is an important way for Ron Wyden to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. Too much email? . Click here to If you no longer want to hear from Ron Wyden, . Questions or concerns? . |

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: The difference between winning and losing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH |
body:
My focus continues to be fighting for families like yours.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186161/336083960/-1829049351?refcode=jtk836-fr-nd-v2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NmVkNjkwOS04OWE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4mIoeonnlRlSKf4IGqkZ1GSy83MLcDqRYNCNusgQv-E=)

I’ll be honest,
I'd prefer not to send you emails asking for money. My focus continues to be fighting for families like yours.
However, my campaign just authorized a $13 million ad investment that we’ll need to get our message out and fight back against GOP attacks, and we don't have all of the money yet. **That's bad under normal circumstances, but with the GOP spending more against me than any other Senate Democrat in the country, we need to stay on track to make this critical ad buy.**
As I said, asking for money is one of my least favorite things to do, but **Mitch McConnell and Republicans across the country have spent over $7.5 million to unseat me.** And if we don't stay on track, we could relinquish vital ad inventory to far-right Republicans who are out to smear my reputation. In a purple state like New Hampshire, that could be the difference between winning or losing.
That's why I need your help to stay on track, friend. **[Will you please donate what you can right now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186162/336083961/-947982778?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk836-fr-nd-v2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NmVkNjkwOS04OWE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4mIoeonnlRlSKf4IGqkZ1GSy83MLcDqRYNCNusgQv-E=)**

I know it's easy to think that a grassroots donation won't make a dent. But I'm counting on grassroots supporters like you to power our campaign, and your $5 makes a huge difference.

Maggie Hassan

name: Alex Walker
subject: I’ll defeat Lauren Boebert
body:
Hi,
I’m Alex, and I’m running for Congress against the most deranged, brainwashed, dumpster-fire-of-a-human in Colorado... Congresswoman Lauren Boebert .
I’m asking if you could help us build our team as big as possible by splitting a donation between my campaign and CommonGood. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cgva_aw?refcode=20220320_CGVA_Walker]
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cgva_aw?refcode=20220320_CGVA_Walker [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cgva_aw?refcode=20220320_CGVA_Walker]
Let’s face it: I’m running against a conspiracy theorist who somehow wandered into Congress in a delirious rage. Her racist rants, failed attempts to overthrow the government, and the fact that she gets her policies from QAnon Reddit posts all feed into this epic shit-storm that is politics today.
And all the while, folks like us are left behind to deal with actual, real world problems.
We deserve better: I’m not a sellout. I don’t change my opinions based on what polls well. I stick to my values, and don’t waiver just for a big check or a shout out.
And I’m building a team of skeptics like me who are tired of the BS, the pandering, and the mind-numbing stupidity that people like Boebert have brought to the table.
So I’m asking you to join me. Will you split a donation between our team and CommonGood today so we can take down the national embarrassment that is Lauren Boebert? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cgva_aw?refcode=20220320_CGVA_Walker]
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cgva_2021_mk?refcode=20211212_CGVA_Kelly]DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cgva_aw?refcode=20220320_CGVA_Walker&amount=25]

name: Val Demings
subject: my 27 years of police service
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Val Demings for U
body:
This is an especially important message

---
| | Friend: This is an especially important message about my 27 years of dedicated service as a police officer. As Marco Rubio launches desperate attacks on my law enforcement career, I hope you'll read this to the end.
---
I entered the police academy because I loved my community and wanted to protect it from harm. After graduating at the top of my class, I woke up and put on my uniform every day, ready to risk my life because _ _ I took an oath to protect and serve the people of Florida.
**While I was working the midnight shift, Marco Rubio was plotting his trajectory as a career politician. **I’ll put my record up against his any day – but the problem is, Rubio has the powerful influence of the GOP dark-money machine on his side.
As national Republicans spend millions of dollars on attack ads that question my loyalty to the men and women in law enforcement, I need your help today to set the record straight: [**Will you donate $5 or more to help me reach more voters and flip Florida blue?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186172/336084081/-892300846?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&refcode=jtk2696-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2N2VkNjkwOS04OWE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SmdTrzZKgTpUecKdhQcajJEic953nZnkwXAYWzPp2LM=)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186173/336084109/-892300846?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2696-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2N2VkNjkwOS04OWE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SmdTrzZKgTpUecKdhQcajJEic953nZnkwXAYWzPp2LM=)
Back in 1983, **I was one of only three women in my police academy class**. But after years of hard work and dedication, I rose to the top of the department and became the first woman to serve as Orlando's chief of police.
My story doesn't stop there. When I was appointed chief of police, Orlando was in crisis. The murder rate was at an all-time high, but I brought the department together and built relationships within the community. Standing with the brave men and women of the Orlando Police Department, we reduced violent crime by 40%.
To protect the city of Orlando, it took long, sleepless nights. And if I'm elected to the Senate, I'm ready to spend long nights protecting our right to vote, reproductive rights, access to health care and so much more.
I'll never stop fighting to protect and serve. If you're with me, please donate $5 or whatever you can right now to flip Florida blue.

Here's the best link: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vd_senate-fr-email](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186174/336084110/-892300846?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2696-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2N2VkNjkwOS04OWE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SmdTrzZKgTpUecKdhQcajJEic953nZnkwXAYWzPp2LM=)**
From the bottom of my heart, thank you for joining this grassroots movement.

Val Demings
| | Our grassroots campaign is off to a great start, but in a race as close as this one, I need your help to surge ahead and defend the Senate. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you’re critical to this grassroots campaign. To make sure you’re the first to get important updates, **[click here if you’re on mobile](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186175/336084121/1417698435?refcode=jtk2696-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2N2VkNjkwOS04OWE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SmdTrzZKgTpUecKdhQcajJEic953nZnkwXAYWzPp2LM=&emci=ae35adba-04a6-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&emdi=67ed6909-89a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&ceid=20244100) or text “JOIN” to 77076 to receive text updates.**

After graduating at the top of my class, I woke up and put on my uniform every day, ready to risk my life because I took an oath to protect and serve the people of Florida. While I was working the midnight shift, Marco Rubio was plotting his trajectory as a career politician. I’ll put my record up against his any day – but the problem is, Rubio has the powerful influence of the GOP dark-money machine on his side. As national Republicans spend millions of dollars on attack ads that question my loyalty to the men and women in law enforcement, I need your help today to set the record straight: = = Back in 1983, I was one of only three women in my police academy class. But after years of hard work and dedication, I rose to the top of the department and became the first woman to serve as Orlando's chief of police. My story doesn't stop there. When I was appointed chief of police, Orlando was in crisis. The murder rate was at an all-time high, but I brought the department together and built relationships within the community. Standing with the brave men and women of the Orlando Police Department, we reduced violent crime by 40%. To protect the city of Orlando, it took long, sleepless nights. And if I'm elected to the Senate, I'm ready to spend long nights protecting our right to vote, reproductive rights, access to health care and so much more. I'll never stop fighting to protect and serve. If you're with me, please donate $5 or whatever you can right now to flip Florida blue. Here's the best link: = From the bottom of my heart, thank you for joining this grassroots movement. Val Demings | | Our grassroots campaign is off to a great start, but in a race as close as this one, I need your help to surge ahead and defend the Senate. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you’re critical to this grassroots campaign. To make sure you’re the first to get important updates, or text “JOIN” to 77076 to receive text updates. & . I’m relying on grassroots supporters like you, donating to emails like this, to help me defeat Marco Rubio and stop Mitch McConnell from flipping the Senate. So, please consider =. You can also click here to or click here to . Val Demings for U.S. Senate P. O. Box 536926 Orlando, FL 32853 United States Paid for by Val Demings for U.S. Senate

Friends , our fundraising deadline ends TOMORROW AT MIDNIGHT and we’re short of where we need to be to hit our goals for the month. The timing is NOT GREAT and Mark needs your help. Can you PLEASE split a contribution today between Mark Kelly and Seth Moulton? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-kelly?refcode=20220320_MK_split]
Things are really heating up...
FIRST: A Mitch-McConnell-backed dark money group started spending millions of dollars to defeat Mark Kelly.
THEN: One of our opponents unleashed a $10 million super PAC to beat us.
AND THEN: Trump told his supporters that beating Mark was one of the most important things they can do this year.
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-kelly?refcode=20220320_MK_split [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-kelly?refcode=20220320_MK_split]

body:
Team, we wanted to make sure you saw this recent profile of Nan! If you didn't, check it out below:
**From political volunteer to Dayton mayor: How Democrat Nan Whaley rose to run for Ohio governor**
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186726/336086708/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)**
_**COLUMBUS, Ohio**_ \-- Nan Whaley has been in politics for about half her life – first as a volunteer, then a staffer, and finally as an elected official rising to become mayor of Dayton for eight years.
Propelled into the spotlight by a 2019 mass shooting, the 46-year-old lifelong Democrat, who left the mayor’s office last year, is now running for governor a second time.
Those who know Whaley -- former colleagues and aides, friends, and a former boss -- painted a similar picture of her in interviews with cleveland.com: gregarious, straightforward, compassionate and driven.
“She can be very generous and warm (and) caring. But she can also be very tough. She’s very loyal, and she expects other people to be loyal,” said Montgomery County Auditor Karl Keith, who gave Whaley her first job out of college. “She doesn’t tolerate laziness very much from other people. She has a hard time dealing with that because she follows through on things, and she’s very hard-working and expects everyone else to be the same.”
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186727/336086709/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)****
**Childhood**
Born and raised in Mooresville, Indiana, Her father was a General Motors autoworker, while her mother worked in a laundromat and became Mooresville’s clerk-treasurer.
Whaley said in an interview that her oldest memory dates back to when she was four years old and saw her mother getting upset watching a 1980 presidential debate between Democrat Jimmy Carter and Republican Ronald Reagan.
“I was like, ‘Why are you upset?’” Whaley said. “She said, ‘Oh, well, you know, Ronald Reagan is gonna win this election, and that’s going to be terrible for our family.’ And sure enough, you know, a year later, my dad got laid off.”
In high school, Whaley went to an all-girls Catholic boarding school about an hour and a half away. The Franciscan nuns who ran the school, Whaley said, taught her how women can lead, as well as perseverance and how to get things done.
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186728/336086710/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)****
**Early days in Dayton**
Whaley said she accepted a sizable scholarship to attend the University of Dayton, even though her Hoosier parents were initially “not pleased” that she moved to Ohio in 1994 (though through 1997 she went back to Indiana during summer breaks).
At UD, she earned a bachelor’s degree in chemistry. As the first member of her family to graduate from college, she said, her family prioritized “bankable” degrees in subjects such as science or business. (Whaley later earned a master of public administration degree from Wright State University in 2009).
But while attending college, Whaley also began taking the bus to downtown Dayton to volunteer at the local Democratic headquarters. The summer after her junior year, she accepted a job as executive director of the Montgomery County Democratic Party -- a job she kept after college.
When Keith was appointed county auditor in 2000, his first hire was Whaley to be his administrative assistant. Keith said he appreciated her organizational and communication skills and her “contagious” enthusiasm.
Keith said Whaley is “very competitive,” even with herself. “As a candidate, she will set goals about how many phone calls to make in one day or how many stops she needs to make, and she keeps track of those things in her head,” he said. “She’s very driven to reach her goals -- daily, monthly, weekly, whatever.”
Darius Beckham, who worked as an aide for Whaley as mayor, also said his former boss is “a worker bee” who keeps a busy schedule.
“I don’t know how she does it,” said Beckham, now chief policy aide for Dayton Mayor Jeffrey Mims. “But Nan is always on the move.”
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186729/336086711/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)****
**Candidate Whaley**
While Whaley volunteered to help on campaigns, she said she didn’t think of running for public office herself until her mid-20s, when EMILY’s List, a group that works to elect Democratic women who support abortion rights, approached her about running for Congress in 2004.
“They said, ‘We want a first-time candidate that has no baggage ...that can run against this first-term Republican, and you just have to raise a million dollars,’” Whaley said. “I come from a working-class background – like, raising a million dollars at that time, you might have said, ‘all you need to do is go to the moon.’”
Whaley passed on their offer, but instead, she ran for the Dayton City Commission in 2005. Even though Whaley was not wealthy, not related to a single person in Dayton at the time, single, and renting a house, she became the youngest woman ever elected a city commissioner.
Whaley said Dayton city commission was the only office she’s sought where others didn’t ask her to run.
“You don’t want to run for something if it’s only you (who is) thinking of running,” she said. “Allies are an important part of this work.”
Matt Joseph, a Democrat who served with Whaley on the city commission for years, said Whaley had a “very collaborative” style of getting things done.
If Whaley wanted to propose something new -- often, some policy idea she picked up while attending a national conference -- Joseph said she would call him and the other three commissioners to discuss it, then eventually send a bullet-point list to their staffers. “So by the time something came up for a vote, we all knew a lot about it,” Joseph said.
Whaley said being an elected official is “much harder” than being a behind-the-scenes staffer. “You have to really listen to people,” she said. “I learned you have to work with others you may not agree with this time -- like, this was a really big change for me...You’ve just got to put yourself out there in a very direct way that can be -- you know, you have to be pretty vulnerable.”
Keith said Whaley has become “more confident in herself and in her abilities” since she worked for him.
“She has great political instincts, and she enjoys politics,” Keith said. “But now she understands policy issues as well as anyone. She grasps things quickly. She retains things -- statistics and details about policy issues. You know, just it’s amazing to me.”
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186730/336086712/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)****
**Mayor of Dayton**
Whaley was elected mayor of Dayton in 2013, defeating the incumbent mayor, independent Gary Leitzell, and Democrat A.J. Wagner, a former Montgomery County common pleas judge and auditor. Whaley ran the city’s second most expensive campaign for mayor, raising almost $365,000 and spending nearly $412,000, according to the Dayton Daily News. Whaley won every precinct in majority-Black West Dayton (where she lives), while Wagner won most of majority-white East Dayton, the newspaper found.
Dayton has a powerful city manager, meaning the mayor of Dayton’s duties are often ceremonial. Leitzell considered the mayor’s job a part-time position. But Whaley actively used the job to push for several significant policy changes.
Whaley successfully convinced Dayton voters to approve a plan providing free preschool to every family with a 4-year-old, paid for by the city’s first income-tax increase in 32 years. Her strategy to fight the opioid epidemic in Dayton -- which included a needle-exchange program and a mobile crisis response team -- won her national attention.
In 2017, she ran for re-election unopposed -- marking Dayton’s first uncontested mayoral race since 1969, when voters began electing mayors separately from other city commissioners.
But Whaley’s defining moment as mayor came in 2019, when a gunman opened fire outside a Dayton bar, killing nine and wounding 17 others.
Whaley not only helped rally Daytonians in the wake of the shooting; she suddenly became the focus of national attention, particularly after getting in a bickering match with then-President Donald Trump. The spat led Whaley to receive a barrage of angry and sometimes threatening messages.
Whaley unapologetically used the tragedy and subsequent media attention to call for stricter gun-control laws, though her proposals went nowhere in Ohio’s Republican-dominated state legislature.
Beckham said he saw how the shooting “hit home” for her.
“Nan listened. She met with the families (of the victims), she went to every service, just about, for those who lost their lives that day,” Beckham said.
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186731/336086713/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)****
**Running for governor**
Whaley said she first thought about running for Ohio governor after Dayton’s preschool initiative passed in 2016.
“I (didn’t) really think about (running for) governor, frankly, until somebody said, ‘Hey, have you thought about doing this?’” Whaley said. “And then, you know, (it got) stuck in my head.”
The following year, Whaley announced a run for governor in 2018. But the Democratic field was crowded, and after former Ohio Attorney General Richard Cordray entered the race, Whaley dropped out.
Cordray eventually lost the 2018 general election to Republican Mike DeWine, whom Whaley has long known -- both because DeWine lives in nearby Greene County and because he was friends with Whaley’s late father-in-law, longtime Dayton-area radio personality John “Bucks” Braun.
Whaley was already being talked about as a potential 2022 gubernatorial candidate in 2019 when her profile was raised even higher by the unexpected publicity she received after the 2019 shooting.
Since entering the 2022 gubernatorial race last year, Whaley has campaigned on raising the state’s minimum wage to $15 per hour and creating a statewide universal pre-K program, among other things.
She’s set to face former Cincinnati Mayor John Cranley, a personal friend, in Ohio’s Democratic primary on May 3. If she wins the primary and the November general election, Whaley would be the first woman elected governor in Ohio history.
Even though Whaley hasn’t previously won statewide office, Keith said she well-suited to running for governor.
“I’ve been doing this for 30 or 40 years, and she’s one of those few candidates that I know who really, really enjoys the grind of a campaign when it comes to fundraising and meeting the public and traveling across the state,” he said.
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186732/336086714/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)****
**Criticism**
Whaley has, of course, faced her share of criticism while in public office. After calling out several Dayton-area Republican lawmakers by name for having “pretty bad votes on guns,” some of those legislators accused Whaley of “politicizing” the issue.
Beckham said that the criticism Whaley received as mayor mainly revolved around limits to her capacity to promptly address a vast number of issues. “Especially when it comes to a city, the number-one people they think to call is the mayor’s office,” Beckham said. “And things aren’t fixed fast.”
Keith said Whaley had attracted her share of opponents, citing her “strong personality” and “difficult positions” on issues such as guns and LGBTQIA rights.
“She has her detractors, no question about it,” Keith said. “And I think that’s because she lets you know where she stands.”
Joseph recalled a press conference he and Whaley held in the summer of 2020 to call for police reforms in Dayton in the wake of the murder of Minneapolis resident George Floyd. As they spoke, he said, a young woman stood nearby who was visibly skeptical that they were serious about making changes to the police.
“Nan went up to her and talked to her for I don’t know how long after the press conference is over, and I think actually recruited her to be part of the police reform effort,” Joseph said.
Beyond Whaley’s policies, she received negative media coverage last year over revelations that the FBI suspected her in 2013 and 2014 of accepting tens of thousands of dollars in bribes from a contractor around the time she was first elected mayor.
Whaley called the claims “baseless and categorically untrue,” and the federal corruption case was closed without any charges filed against her.
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186733/336086715/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)****
**At home with Nan**
Outside of politics, Whaley says she likes to exercise, practice yoga, walk her dog Louis, and hang out with neighbors. She tries to visit her parents, who now live near Cincinnati, every Friday.
Whaley and her husband, Sam Braun, are also part of a famously close-knit community of neighbors on Wroe Avenue, a street of middle-class houses in Dayton’s Five Oaks neighborhood.
Marty Gehres, Dayton’s 31-year-old municipal clerk, grew up across Wroe Avenue from Whaley and now owns a house a few doors down. He’s known Whaley since he was 10 when she -- then a college student -- was his babysitter. Today, Gehres is a frequent visitor to Whaley’s porch, as he’s good friends with Braun.
“I think she’s pretty much an open book,” Gehres said of Whaley. “She’s not a typical politician that, you know, there’s a facade out in public and a different one at home. She’s pretty much the same anytime you interact with her.”
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43186734/336086716/-306467447?refcode=nw_em_220320_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=)****

* * *

PO Box 2815, Dayton, OH 45401
This campaign is powered by supporters like you, but if you'd like to unsubscribe, [click here](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/189ecb01-f2a6-ec11-a22a-281878b85110/4e9c0fb9-90a8-ec11-a22a-281878b85110?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTljMGZiOS05MGE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TM6kCP3OKEQkMXcKSn41qq_3xhwr5Ac2IFqLzH1CMCA=&id=127257013).

She’s very loyal, and she expects other people to be loyal,” said Montgomery County Auditor Karl Keith, who gave Whaley her first job out of college. “She doesn’t tolerate laziness very much from other people. She has a hard time dealing with that because she follows through on things, and she’s very hard-working and expects everyone else to be the same.” **= Childhood Born and raised in Mooresville, Indiana, Her father was a General Motors autoworker, while her mother worked in a laundromat and became Mooresville’s clerk-treasurer. Whaley said in an interview that her oldest memory dates back to when she was four years old and saw her mother getting upset watching a 1980 presidential debate between Democrat Jimmy Carter and Republican Ronald Reagan. “I was like, ‘Why are you upset?’” Whaley said. “She said, ‘Oh, well, you know, Ronald Reagan is gonna win this election, and that’s going to be terrible for our family.’ And sure enough, you know, a year later, my dad got laid off.” In high school, Whaley went to an all-girls Catholic boarding school about an hour and a half away. The Franciscan nuns who ran the school, Whaley said, taught her how women can lead, as well as perseverance and how to get things done. = Early days in Dayton Whaley said she accepted a sizable scholarship to attend the University of Dayton, even though her Hoosier parents were initially “not pleased” that she moved to Ohio in 1994 (though through 1997 she went back to Indiana during summer breaks). At UD, she earned a bachelor’s degree in chemistry. As the first member of her family to graduate from college, she said, her family prioritized “bankable” degrees in subjects such as science or business. (Whaley later earned a master of public administration degree from Wright State University in 2009). But while attending college, Whaley also began taking the bus to downtown Dayton to volunteer at the local Democratic headquarters. The summer after her junior year, she accepted a job as executive director of the Montgomery County Democratic Party -- a job she kept after college. When Keith was appointed county auditor in 2000, his first hire was Whaley to be his administrative assistant. Keith said he appreciated her organizational and communication skills and her “contagious” enthusiasm. Keith said Whaley is “very competitive,” even with herself. “As a candidate, she will set goals about how many phone calls to make in one day or how many stops she needs to make, and she keeps track of those things in her head,” he said. “She’s very driven to reach her goals -- daily, monthly, weekly, whatever.” Darius Beckham, who worked as an aide for Whaley as mayor, also said his former boss is “a worker bee” who keeps a busy schedule. “I don’t know how she does it,” said Beckham, now chief policy aide for Dayton Mayor Jeffrey Mims. “But Nan is always on the move.” = Candidate Whaley While Whaley volunteered to help on campaigns, she said she didn’t think of running for public office herself until her mid-20s, when EMILY’s List, a group that works to elect Democratic women who support abortion rights, approached her about running for Congress in 2004. “They said, ‘We want a first-time candidate that has no baggage ...that can run against this first-term Republican, and you just have to raise a million dollars,’” Whaley said. “I come from a working-class background – like, raising a million dollars at that time, you might have said, ‘all you need to do is go to the moon.’” Whaley passed on their offer, but instead, she ran for the Dayton City Commission in 2005. Even though Whaley was not wealthy, not related to a single person in Dayton at the time, single, and renting a house, she became the youngest woman ever elected a city commissioner. Whaley said Dayton city commission was the only office she’s sought where others didn’t ask her to run. “You don’t want to run for something if it’s only you (who is) thinking of running,” she said. “Allies are an important part of this work.” Matt Joseph, a Democrat who served with Whaley on the city commission for years, said Whaley had a “very collaborative” style of getting things done. If Whaley wanted to propose something new -- often, some policy idea she picked up while attending a national conference -- Joseph said she would call him and the other three commissioners to discuss it, then eventually send a bullet-point list to their staffers. “So by the time something came up for a vote, we all knew a lot about it,” Joseph said. Whaley said being an elected official is “much harder” than being a behind-the-scenes staffer. “You have to really listen to people,” she said. “I learned you have to work with others you may not agree with this time -- like, this was a really big change for me...You’ve just got to put yourself out there in a very direct way that can be -- you know, you have to be pretty vulnerable.” Keith said Whaley has become “more confident in herself and in her abilities” since she worked for him. “She has great political instincts, and she enjoys politics,” Keith said. “But now she understands policy issues as well as anyone. She grasps things quickly. She retains things -- statistics and details about policy issues. You know, just it’s amazing to me.” = Mayor of Dayton Whaley was elected mayor of Dayton in 2013, defeating the incumbent mayor, independent Gary Leitzell, and Democrat A.J. Wagner, a former Montgomery County common pleas judge and auditor. Whaley ran the city’s second most expensive campaign for mayor, raising almost $365,000 and spending nearly $412,000, according to the Dayton Daily News. Whaley won every precinct in majority-Black West Dayton (where she lives), while Wagner won most of majority-white East Dayton, the newspaper found. Dayton has a powerful city manager, meaning the mayor of Dayton’s duties are often ceremonial. Leitzell considered the mayor’s job a part-time position. But Whaley actively used the job to push for several significant policy changes. Whaley successfully convinced Dayton voters to approve a plan providing free preschool to every family with a 4-year-old, paid for by the city’s first income-tax increase in 32 years. Her strategy to fight the opioid epidemic in Dayton -- which included a needle-exchange program and a mobile crisis response team -- won her national attention. In 2017, she ran for re-election unopposed -- marking Dayton’s first uncontested mayoral race since 1969, when voters began electing mayors separately from other city commissioners. But Whaley’s defining moment as mayor came in 2019, when a gunman opened fire outside a Dayton bar, killing nine and wounding 17 others. Whaley not only helped rally Daytonians in the wake of the shooting; she suddenly became the focus of national attention, particularly after getting in a bickering match with then-President Donald Trump. The spat led Whaley to receive a barrage of angry and sometimes threatening messages. Whaley unapologetically used the tragedy and subsequent media attention to call for stricter gun-control laws, though her proposals went nowhere in Ohio’s Republican-dominated state legislature. Beckham said he saw how the shooting “hit home” for her. “Nan listened. She met with the families (of the victims), she went to every service, just about, for those who lost their lives that day,” Beckham said. = Running for governor Whaley said she first thought about running for Ohio governor after Dayton’s preschool initiative passed in 2016. “I (didn’t) really think about (running for) governor, frankly, until somebody said, ‘Hey, have you thought about doing this?’” Whaley said. “And then, you know, (it got) stuck in my head.” The following year, Whaley announced a run for governor in 2018. But the Democratic field was crowded, and after former Ohio Attorney General Richard Cordray entered the race, Whaley dropped out. Cordray eventually lost the 2018 general election to Republican Mike DeWine, whom Whaley has long known -- both because DeWine lives in nearby Greene County and because he was friends with Whaley’s late father-in-law, longtime Dayton-area radio personality John “Bucks” Braun. Whaley was already being talked about as a potential 2022 gubernatorial candidate in 2019 when her profile was raised even higher by the unexpected publicity she received after the 2019 shooting. Since entering the 2022 gubernatorial race last year, Whaley has campaigned on raising the state’s minimum wage to $15 per hour and creating a statewide universal pre-K program, among other things. She’s set to face former Cincinnati Mayor John Cranley, a personal friend, in Ohio’s Democratic primary on May 3. If she wins the primary and the November general election, Whaley would be the first woman elected governor in Ohio history. Even though Whaley hasn’t previously won statewide office, Keith said she well-suited to running for governor. “I’ve been doing this for 30 or 40 years, and she’s one of those few candidates that I know who really, really enjoys the grind of a campaign when it comes to fundraising and meeting the public and traveling across the state,” he said. = Criticism Whaley has, of course, faced her share of criticism while in public office. After calling out several Dayton-area Republican lawmakers by name for having “pretty bad votes on guns,” some of those legislators accused Whaley of “politicizing” the issue. Beckham said that the criticism Whaley received as mayor mainly revolved around limits to her capacity to promptly address a vast number of issues. “Especially when it comes to a city, the number-one people they think to call is the mayor’s office,” Beckham said. “And things aren’t fixed fast.” Keith said Whaley had attracted her share of opponents, citing her “strong personality” and “difficult positions” on issues such as guns and LGBTQIA rights. “She has her detractors, no question about it,” Keith said. “And I think that’s because she lets you know where she stands.” Joseph recalled a press conference he and Whaley held in the summer of 2020 to call for police reforms in Dayton in the wake of the murder of Minneapolis resident George Floyd. As they spoke, he said, a young woman stood nearby who was visibly skeptical that they were serious about making changes to the police. “Nan went up to her and talked to her for I don’t know how long after the press conference is over, and I think actually recruited her to be part of the police reform effort,” Joseph said. Beyond Whaley’s policies, she received negative media coverage last year over revelations that the FBI suspected her in 2013 and 2014 of accepting tens of thousands of dollars in bribes from a contractor around the time she was first elected mayor. Whaley called the claims “baseless and categorically untrue,” and the federal corruption case was closed without any charges filed against her. = At home with Nan Outside of politics, Whaley says she likes to exercise, practice yoga, walk her dog Louis, and hang out with neighbors. She tries to visit her parents, who now live near Cincinnati, every Friday. Whaley and her husband, Sam Braun, are also part of a famously close-knit community of neighbors on Wroe Avenue, a street of middle-class houses in Dayton’s Five Oaks neighborhood. Marty Gehres, Dayton’s 31-year-old municipal clerk, grew up across Wroe Avenue from Whaley and now owns a house a few doors down. He’s known Whaley since he was 10 when she -- then a college student -- was his babysitter. Today, Gehres is a frequent visitor to Whaley’s porch, as he’s good friends with Braun. “I think she’s pretty much an open book,” Gehres said of Whaley. “She’s not a typical politician that, you know, there’s a facade out in public and a different one at home. She’s pretty much the same anytime you interact with her.” = Paid for by Friends of Nan Whaley PO Box 2815, Dayton, OH 45401 This campaign is powered by supporters like you, but if you'd like to unsubscribe, .

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: The DCCC raised $19,300,000
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC) raised $19.3
million in February. Yes, $19.3 million in one month.

The DCCC recruits corporate Democrats and protects establishment
incumbents while taking money from Wall Street and corporate PACs. A lot
of that money goes towards stopping progressive legislation like Medicare
for All and a Green New Deal.

Justice Democrats doesn’t take a cent of money from corporate PACs because
we’re fighting for the people, NOT corporate profits. We’re running
working-class progressive primary challengers to challenge the status quo.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/98114?refcode=em20220320-22479&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=22479%2E1203867%2EEjqmlU ]This month, we’re trying to raise just 1.1% of what the DCCC raised
last month. Peter,
now is the perfect time to make a
contribution to fuel our goal to elect the next working-class progressives
to Congress. Can you donate today?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/98114?refcode=em20220320-22479&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=22479%2E1203867%2EEjqmlU ]Contribute »

We need a Democratic Party that fights for its voters, not corporate
donors. JD does the hard work of recruiting, training, fundraising, and
organizing with bold, progressive candidates.

On one side of this intra-party ideas battle, you have the DCCC, which
raises huge sums of money to support establishment candidates that keep
the Democratic Party status quo alive.

Then there’s our side. We’re building a solid base of progressives in
Congress fighting for Medicare for All, racial justice, a Green New Deal —
and we’re doing it with an incredibly low budget. But the reality is we
could be doing more if we had more resources to go against the DCCC.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/98114?refcode=em20220320-22479&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=22479%2E1203867%2EEjqmlU ]Today, we need you to help us raise just 1.1% of what the DCCC raised
last month so we can run strong primary challenges to corporate
incumbents. Can we count on you to make a donation?

All of your support helps us transform the Democratic Party and make it
more responsive to the needs of the people.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/98114?refcode=em20220320-22479&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1023&akid=22479%2E1203867%2EEjqmlU ]Contribute »

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1024&akid=22479%2E1203867%2EEjqmlU ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1025&akid=22479%2E1203867%2EEjqmlU ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20220320-22479-footer&t=1026&refcode2=22479_1203867_EjqmlU&akid=22479%2E1203867%2EEjqmlU

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1027&akid=22479%2E1203867%2EEjqmlU

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226
Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: BetoORourke.com
subject: Why we send so many emails
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
A donation today will help ensure we have the resources to keep Beto on the road, go toe-to-toe with Abbott's mega-donors, and defeat this failed governor once and for all.
#
We know we send a lot of emails, but it's only because this race to elect Beto as Texas' next governor couldn't be more urgent. **The future of our state is on the line.**
Our team is working hard to make sure we have the grassroots resources we need to travel across Texas, advertise online, hire campaign organizers, and compete with Abbott's $50 million in the bank. _That's where you come in._
To defeat Abbott and his dangerous, extreme policies, we're going to build the largest grassroots campaign Texas has ever seen. We'll need the support of as many people as possible — including you! **But according to our records, it looks like you have yet to chip in to power this campaign.**
We understand you may not have been in a position to donate before, but with our end-of-quarter deadline coming up quickly, we're hoping we can count on support today.
**[Will you consider making your first donation to help elect Beto as Texas' next Governor today? It's donations of $3, $10, and $25 that are powering this campaign.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/54070?refcode=ema20220320-181283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=181283%2E444211%2EfQw-MI)**
**[Contribute $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/54070?refcode=ema20220320-181283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=181283%2E444211%2EfQw-MI)**
Beto believes in running a people-powered campaign, and here's what that actually means: We don't accept a cent from corporate PACs or their special interests — 100% of our donations come from folks like you who believe in our vision for a bigger, bolder Texas.
We took a look at our notes, and we can stay on track with today's fundraising goal if everyone reading this email who hasn't chipped in before makes their first donation of $3 or more right now.
**[If you're able, please make your first $3 donation today to ensure we have the resources to keep Beto on the road, go toe-to-toe with Abbott's mega-donors, and defeat Abbott once and for all.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/54070?refcode=ema20220320-181283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=5&akid=181283%2E444211%2EfQw-MI)**
Everyone in Texas deserves a big, unifying vision for this state. Big enough for all of us to live to our full potential.
Thank you,
Beto for Texas
_*This data only reflects donations made online with this email address. If you’ve made a previous contribution via check or with a different email address, you may not see it here — but know that Beto and our entire team are deeply grateful for your support._

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bft-em-footer?refcode=em20220320-181283-footer&t=6&refcode2=181283_444211_fQw-MI&akid=181283%2E444211%2EfQw-MI)**
---
Email us: info@betofortexas.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Grassroots supporters like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Beto for Texas, PO Box 302647, Austin, TX 78703.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/bft-receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.betoorourke.com/sign/bft-personal-info/?t=7&akid=181283%2E444211%2EfQw-MI)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/bft-unsubscribe/?t=8&akid=181283%2E444211%2EfQw-MI)

Pol. Adv. Paid for by Beto for Texas.

ConEd is screwing over working families, Peter Willis.
Our utility bills are too high, and big companies like Con Edison are taking in record profits at the expense of families struggling to get by.
Just last week, **ConEd requested to hike rates _by nearly 20 percent_ while threatening shutoffs for thousands of New Yorkers unable to keep up.**
Yesterday, Melanie held a rally with Jonathan Soto, candidate for New York State’s 82nd Assembly District in the Bronx, to fight back and demand a publicly-owned power grid for New York.
[**Can you add your support to call out ConEd and stand with us against corporate greed?**](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnn6M2L4lcb-awdC-BLNjuQ_9XngGgdRcatJjoahlS1q4KyBbCFzG_HG-_n5hniF74U9hkIwcaXbkBfzANPk5n0eV8-7htskk_Y7MobcJNdl523aRaZSZ1CfSI6Zwjdp3Ljm7JwFltFvuolnD5rojuauz_nmhPxMXSf5uT6c1fpjukinNcLi-UI91xJo_eCuUT_6Db3LB0ZWkiza6tMNplY6r66OgpYHUlVmN5dVmTej28zoHp2hUe1ZZj8EbDFtdr7eIs4_4ZvXEXlUa0EoA0f_U-I-Q0ZZnXm8LxJ2aY6Qf4M4GbY9mKJW7dNjDjZ_fdeyswcGK8G6740PQLmRCmyp/3ki/spbmD-x7QW-VMt3iDYgyYA/h1/tP6e_Db_T_5dubRbvZJkmwurYiLlo8UnmSAevNPgUQo)
[Add your name](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnn6M2L4lcb-awdC-BLNjuQ_9XngGgdRcatJjoahlS1q4KyBbCFzG_HG-_n5hniF74U9hkIwcaXbkBfzANPk5n0eEV_7GjlrdgoHDCbJwPjXi1iR7JGXV3EY290RuAVsD42o0EYO5gMbMY9n8qYUE4LGp5h17NU7sxSiOY26m6dnV6-Lq0YwNZCbfP6R3-drsyfQN0qnrLgpIQRyzgxRpUJJOFAGjlEGtFoOAHOtbneCWx2Q-iBmNkrrG7id--FuroPMXI8HRgWnCQ9nlvwBdeoS5XMamLlKKDHHZ_PSfO5PbLkJR-qmPS4YwtJreYpsujhwOogjFRM920ea3uB-8fE3/3ki/spbmD-x7QW-VMt3iDYgyYA/h2/qiBRuxBYV0nOeLOqyXf1n6OZaJNj1ad57GYWMVwC1E0)

More than 400,000 New Yorkers are at least two months behind on their energy bills — yet ConEdison was able to report billions of dollars in profits last fiscal year. Melanie and Jonathan are proud to be the only candidates in their respective races ready to take on ConEdison and fight for a more sustainable, equitable energy future.
_“As long as our energy is controlled by corporations, we won’t have any choice in how much we pay and what we’re paying for — which means that companies like ConEdison can continue ripping off New Yorkers and contributing to the climate crisis.**Publicly-owned power will drive costs down and keep the lights on, and it can set us on a path to a greener future.** ” — Melanie D’Arrigo_

clean_body: | | | | ConEd is screwing over working families, Peter Willis. Our utility bills are too high, and big companies like Con Edison are taking in record profits at the expense of families struggling to get by. Just last week, ConEd requested to hike rates by nearly 20 percent while threatening shutoffs for thousands of New Yorkers unable to keep up. Yesterday, Melanie held a rally with Jonathan Soto, candidate for New York State’s 82nd Assembly District in the Bronx, to fight back and demand a publicly-owned power grid for New York. More than 400,000 New Yorkers are at least two months behind on their energy bills — yet ConEdison was able to report billions of dollars in profits last fiscal year. Melanie and Jonathan are proud to be the only candidates in their respective races ready to take on ConEdison and fight for a more sustainable, equitable energy future. “As long as our energy is controlled by corporations, we won’t have any choice in how much we pay and what we’re paying for — which means that companies like ConEdison can continue ripping off New Yorkers and contributing to the climate crisis.Publicly-owned power will drive costs down and keep the lights on, and it can set us on a path to a greener future. ” — Melanie D’Arrigo Thanks, Team Melanie Melanie D'Arrigo is a mother of three and healthcare advocate running for New York's 3rd District on Medicare for All and the Green New Deal. Our movement isn't taking a dime of corporate PAC money, which means we need your support. | | PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF MELANIE DARRIGO (not by corporations or lobbyists) Friends of Melanie D'Arrigo P.O. clean_body: This has gone beyond political opinions: this is a threat to our public health. | | | Team Ruiz hopes that you and your family are safe and healthy. Public health across the country is our top concern during this unprecedented time. To view the most up to date CDC COVID-19 safety recommendations, And if you have a moment, please read our message below. I’m DISGUSTED. Attacks on Dr. Fauci are beyond politics: they threaten our entire public health apparatus. Dr. Fauci’s long-time colleagues have even said this is the most visibly hurt they’ve seen him – he needs our support now more than ever! | As a Congressman and as a doctor myself, I’ve seen the devastating impacts of COVID-19 on our country. I can confidently say that Dr. Fauci’s leadership has been essential in leading us through this pandemic. But anti-science Republicans like Rand Paul will do ANYTHING to destroy his reputation and spread misinformation. So it’s up to us to protect Dr. Fauci’s legacy and continue our fight against COVID-19. Thank you for your support, Dr. Raul Ruiz Cincinnati.com: Rand Paul calls Anthony Fauci a 'menace;' latest in increasingly personal feud | PO Box 3433 Palm Desert, CA 92261 PAID FOR BY DR. RAUL RUIZ FOR CONGRESS COMMITTEE Please read the following message from Dr. Raul Ruiz: I'm so thankful for your support. With President Biden and Vice President Harris in the White House, we have a clear path forward. But we can't get complacent. We have to keep fighting if we're going to undo the damage Donald Trump has done. | Together we can protect access to health care, expand Social Security, and fight for vital institutions like the Post Office. Because it's what's right. As [1] of just [3] Democratic physicians in Congress, it's more important than ever that I am able to fight for our most vulnerable communities. But I need you in this fight with me: From the emergency room to the halls of Congress, it's my duty to do as much good as I can. Can I count on you to help me keep fighting in Congress? Friend,
---
With Biden plummeting in the polls and Pelosi’s majority just 5 seats away from falling apart, Republicans have never had a better opportunity to sweep Congress than this year’s midterms.
---
But we don’t _just_ want to take back the House with any old Republicans, we need true America First conservatives in the halls of Congress who will always put YOU first.
---
**That’s why I promised my good friend Cassy Garcia in Texas’ 28th congressional district that I would do whatever I could to help her win big ahead of her runoff election and FLIP this district from Pelosi’s control come November.**

clean_body: We don’t want just any old Republicans in our new majority. | Friend, With Biden plummeting in the polls and Pelosi’s majority just 5 seats away from falling apart, Republicans have never had a better opportunity to sweep Congress than this year’s midterms. But we don’t just want to take back the House with any old Republicans, we need true America First conservatives in the halls of Congress who will always put YOU first. That’s why I promised my good friend Cassy Garcia in Texas’ 28th congressional district that I would do whatever I could to help her win big ahead of her runoff election and FLIP this district from Pelosi’s control come November. Cassy and I share the belief that to once again achieve American greatness, we must first stamp out the radical Left’s disastrous agenda. Cassy is committed to securing our border and stopping Joe Biden’s record-high inflation crisis. She has the endorsement of Senator Ted Cruz and was even appointed by President Trump to advance Hispanic prosperity. That’s why I’m honored to support Cassy in her fight to win Texas’ 28th district so that she can join me in fighting back against Pelosi’s radical Democrats in Congress. But she can’t win this fight all on her own... So, can I count on you to join me in supporting Cassy? Thank you, Wesley Hunt Former U.S. Army Captain Republican for Congress 1707 1/2 Post Oak Blvd. #525 Houston, TX 77056 Paid for by Hunt for Congress. There are far too many financial pressures burdening working New York families today – **but one so often overlooked is the debilitating cost of child care. **

That cost is higher than ever – and so too is the need for it. We made great strides when President Biden and Vice President Harris enhanced the child tax credit, but their efforts to support children and working families through the American Families Plan were obstructed by Republicans at every turn.

New York parents shouldn't have to choose between going to work or having to stay home because of the crippling expense of child care. It should be a basic right for every family – especially in the devastating wake of the pandemic, which hit children and caregivers the hardest.
**_That's why I called for universal child care across the state at a press conference today._** And I will not rest until quality care is an accessible and affordable reality for every last New Yorker who needs it.

**But I need your help:****I'm looking for 2,517 more responses to this survey before midnight tonight, and it looks like yours is still missing. So please, let me know if you stand with me on this important issue.**
| | **Do you support universal child care?**
---

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43177380/336072507/27414565?refcode=em_eng_2022.03.20_b1_tq_all&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNWY5MzZjYy03OWE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6Mlm1rMj45zL8YW22og97zC4qpUBL3No-iBtuj-pH3U=) The words above in our makeshift Wordle are just a few that come to mind when we think of the women we know, and those who have shaped our history. While we celebrate the progress that’s been made since our nation’s founding, we must remember that women are still fighting tough battles today. body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43182697/336075027/-1638671833?refcode=fl_email_fr_20220320costs2_dbc4c_allop_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTY0ZjBjMy03ZWE4LWVjMTEtYTIyYS0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RSmAOSp4ug2UTV84yfdY90ZBoTaf2NXJ-nVJnI9CjUk=)
